module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:28 — 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern.' Previously blocked as a rule-27 violation. Now in-scope-by-design under the Connections carve-out added 2026-05-03: a bare harvest sentence as a separate paragraph after the Connections question is design-intentional — the harvest IS the work, not a stage direction. Maintainer block line 86 names the carve-out rationale explicitly ('Connections-as-room-read — deliberate exception to check_pedagogy.md rule 27'). No other cohort-management cues authored into student-facing body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 116–119: 'Leap test' section present with three numbered, verifiable, falsifiable Monday-morning behaviors — (1) enters plan mode on non-trivial multi-file task before approving (falsified by scrollback showing mode-cycle on 2+ files), (2) sends keep-planning-with-feedback push-back referencing specific step number and specific word (falsified by quote of plan's own step text), (3) asks Claude to walk down unresolved branches one question at a time (falsified by Q-and-A sequence in scrollback). All three pass the diagnostic: a working engineer can tell on Monday whether they did it or not.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 128–140: 'Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch' table present with nine rows covering every phase forcing function — P1 blocker, P3 rubber-stamp, P3 generic push-back, P3 softening on regeneration, P4 walk-skip, P4 auto-accept, P5 execution-creep, P5 deflection, Compound skip. Each row names a dominant failure mode and one escape hatch (trainer / Nerd line). Both trainer-led and host-skill-led delivery modes covered. Satisfies rule 47.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced file is a contract row in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 121–126: 'Artefact contracts' table present. Two rows: (1) task-shaping rule file — stable identifier (student-picked location with three named options), produced by Exercise 2, consumed by M4 walk-and-fill Phase 1; (2) personal rules update — stable identifier (CLAUDE.local.md, repo-personal, gitignored), produced by 'Save the rule if it earned itself' prompt, consumed by every future session in this repo + M3. Contract rows satisfy rule 46.

Mood contract 'grounded competence' is honored throughout. The two-reads design pattern lands via doing (not lecture). Key Concepts breadcrumbs ('Assume 90% on first pass', 'Push-backs ship into memory') reinforce the mood. Bridge closes on the leverage horizon without premature resolution. Phase 4 trace mood_score 8 confirms landing.

No punitive framing. 'We all do it' normalizes rubber-stamping; the exercise frames the push-back as a skill to install, not a mistake to correct. Plan-mode approval inflation is named as a structural tell (RLHF), not a moral failure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with 'Reading a plan is finite' — a positive discipline framing. Failure mode ('plan-mode approval inflation') is the negative case the pairing defeats, named AFTER the student has already defeated it (at P5), not in the title.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 inlines the Socratic walk-down behavior as a plain prompt without naming it as a skill. Bridge explicitly defers skill reveal to M3 ('Module 3 reveals that and has you author your first one'). Strategy doc §M2: 'M3 owns first-Skill-use; M2 pre-installing a skill would front-run the teaching moment.' Confirmed consistent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts surfaces: compounding ('Push-backs ship into memory' — Block 1 compounding theme); mirror ('the gap between them is where the plan-reading skill lives' — self-awareness theme); non-agentic-default ('Assumption-silent isn't assumption-free' — explicit assumption-making); 90% theme ('Assume 90% on first pass' breadcrumb). At least three themes covered per rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullets use tactical phrasing with bolded breadcrumbs: '**Push-backs ship into memory.**' and '**Assume 90% on first pass.**' Two breadcrumbs on the most load-bearing concepts. No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Engineer register throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'When a plan is good' is placed before the exercise — it arms the student to do (shows ceiling of what a good plan looks like), consistent with the opener rule for demo/ceiling lectures. 'Where the rule could live' is placed after both exercises as a meta-frame closer that names three patterns the student already heard Claude propose. Placement is correct per the rule: opener = ceiling lecture, closer = meta-frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The main forcing functions are in fenced prompts: P2 prompt includes 'Plan the task I describe below. [...] Detail over brevity.'; P4 prompt includes 'one at a time'; P5 prompt enforces pattern-naming. The compound prompt is fenced with correct carve-out label '*(Claude Code, only if something earned itself)*'. Body prose between prompts is explanatory, not load-bearing forcing functions. Optional refinement prompts (push-backs) loosely in body is covered by rule 16's carve-out for optional moves.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pattern-recognition LOs need n≥2 instances with contrast.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The 'Recognize the pairing as a repeatable design pattern' LO is met by two distinct reads (human push-back read + agent walk-down read) with explicit contrast at P5 ('what the second-pass read surfaced that my two push-backs didn't'). The exercise produces n=2 instances by design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — per phase + close (≥8 required; 7 = facilitator-premium; <7 = REVISE).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 score: 7 (facilitator-premium — caused by room-harvest sentence in self-study context; Connections carve-out makes this design-intentional, not a rule violation; 7 is facilitator-premium not a blocker). Phases 2–4 score: 8. Close score: 8. No phase below 7.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M2 grounded competence must not preempt M3 skill-packaging.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge to M3: 'Module 3 reveals that and has you author your first one.' The second-pass read is inlined as a plain prompt — never named as a skill. The rules file in Extract the task-shaping rule is a .md, not a skill. The automation shapes are named but not built. Grounded competence closes without triggering M3's first-Skill-use teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — plan-mode approval inflation catches reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The teaching moment (approval inflation named after the student defeats it at P5) is structurally enforced: the P5 prompt asks Claude to name the design pattern and compare the two reads. Even a fast operator who skims body prose reaches P5 because it's a fenced prompt. Even a senior engineer who genuinely reads plans carefully encounters 'softening-on-regeneration' at P3 as a teaching moment. The pattern lands across skill variation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module honors the Big Idea framing rather than a different frame.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Reading a plan is finite. Your own read catches some of it, a second agent walking the decision tree catches the rest.' The module opens with the two-reads premise, delivers both reads as exercises, closes with the gap analysis at P5. The 'plan-mode approval inflation' label names the failure mode the pairing defeats — consistent with strategy doc framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — M2 close hands off to M3 open correctly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge: 'Module 3 takes the same move into durable infrastructure: the judges and gates you ship to your team kit catch exactly the kind of branch the second read surfaced today, before a teammate needs to ask. And the move itself turns out to be packageable as a skill; Module 3 reveals that and has you author your first one.' Handoff is accurate: the push-back discipline becomes the gate spec at M3. Skill reveal is deferred to M3 correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-reads before M3 appear in M2 body (lines 63–69: Willison lethal trifecta + OWASP). M2 Prework section (lines 12–13) references M1→M2 readings. The M2→M3 pre-reads need also to appear in M3's Prework section — this is a cross-file check outside this judge's primary surface; noted for technical class.

stealing_the_mood: Phase 1 (Connections): 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern' — self-study persona has no room to harvest with. Design-intentional under Connections carve-out (check_pedagogy.md rule 27, 2026-05-03); facilitator-premium gap, not a rule violation. Score 7 is facilitator-premium (not blocking).
